items.title: Xiaomi 开源 MiMo-V2.5-Pro (1.02T MoE, 强 Agent 编码)
items.summary: 4 月 28 日小米开源 MiMo-V2.5 系列(MIT)。Pro 版 1.02T/42B 激活,混合注意力(128-token 滑窗 : 全局 = 6:1,KV cache ~7×↓),1M 上下文。配 MiMo Code agent harness,厂商基准在 SWE-bench Verified(82 vs 79)/Pro(62 vs 55)/Terminal-Bench 2(73 vs 69)超 Claude Code+Sonnet 4.6。尚无 Ascend 移植。数字均厂商口径,provisional。

items.title: Seed 2.1 Pro 抢先版上线 LMArena (闭源, 编码/Agent)
items.summary: 6 月 19 日 ByteDance 把 Seed-2.1-Pro-Preview 放上 Code Arena 试用:前端编码子榜 1539 Elo、全球第 8,约等于 Claude Opus 4.6。闭源 API、无 Ascend 移植——是标杆而非 NPU 载荷。完整基准随正式版未来几周公布。分析见 Blog → Dispatch 01。

items.title: 新一批开源模型 (6月中): Qwen3 Coder Next · MiniMax M2.7 · Nemotron 3 Ultra
items.summary: 6 月中又一波开放权重发布:Qwen3 Coder Next、MiniMax M2.5/M2.7 Highspeed、NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra 等(新闻聚合,基准/细节待官方)。

items.title: DeepSeek-V4 & GLM-5 run on Ascend 910B (vLLM-Ascend)
items.summary: vLLM-Ascend's 2026 support matrix adds DeepSeek-V4 (custom 910B ops, MTP KV-cache sharding for spec decoding) and GLM-5 (W8A8C8 quant); Qwen3.5 + W8A8 across Qwen3/DeepSeek/MiniMax. MiniMax-M3 not yet listed.

items.title: GLM-5.2 open-sourced (MIT, 1M context)
items.summary: Launched Jun 13 2026; MIT open weights now live on Hugging Face (zai-org/GLM-5.2). 753B params (GLM-5.1-class 744B MoE / 40B active), context 200K→1M, max output 131K, coding-first. No official benchmarks at launch; media cites it edging GPT-5.5 on long-horizon coding (FrontierSWE/SWE-Marathon) at ~1/6 cost — treat as provisional.
items.ascendNote: GLM-5 is supported on vLLM-Ascend (W8A8C8 quant); GLM-5.2 not yet listed by name.

items.title: MiniMax-M3 with MSA sparse attention
items.summary: Open-weight 1M-context model, native multimodal + agentic coding. MSA (blockwise sparse on GQA) cuts attention compute ~28.4x at 1M context. Official report on arXiv.
items.ascendNote: MiniMax family has W8A8/QuaRot on vLLM-Ascend (M2.x); M3 not yet listed by name.

items.title: DeepSeek-V4 (1.6T MoE, 1M context)
items.summary: V4-Pro/Flash with hybrid sparse attention (CSA+HCA), Multi-Token Prediction, and a multi-teacher distillation pipeline over 32T+ tokens. Official tech report PDF on Hugging Face.
items.ascendNote: Supported on Ascend 910B via vLLM-Ascend (custom ops, MTP KV-cache sharding for speculative decoding).

items.title: Agentic RL: a distinct paradigm (survey)
items.summary: Survey reframing agentic RL vs single-turn RLVR — multi-turn credit assignment, tool/environment interaction, long-horizon planning.

items.title: Stable Asynchrony: variance-controlled off-policy RL
items.summary: Controls heavy-tailed importance weights from stale rollouts to keep async/off-policy GRPO stable at high asynchrony.

items.title: Ascend 950PR / 950DT announced (FP8, self-built HBM)
items.summary: Next-gen Ascend with native FP8/MXFP4 and Huawei's own HBM — 950PR (inference) ~Q1 2026, 950DT (training) ~Q4 2026. Opens the door to FP8 RL on Ascend.

items.title: vLLM-Ascend v0.13.0 released
items.summary: Latest Ascend rollout backend — broader model coverage and performance; the rollout engine RL stacks target on NPU.

items.title: DeepSeek-V3.2 + Sparse Attention (DSA)
items.summary: Open-weight frontier model with trainable sparse attention for cheaper long-context; RL post-training continues the R1 lineage.

items.title: GDRO-RL: distributionally robust GRPO
items.summary: Rollout-/Prompt-GDRO reweight toward worst-case prompt distributions on top of GRPO for more robust reasoning RL.

items.title: MindSpeed-RL: Distributed RL Post-Training on Ascend NPU
items.summary: Native distributed GRPO-family RL on Ascend, with experiments up to DeepSeek-R1-MoE-671B on a 384-NPU superpod — the strongest large-scale RL-on-Ascend evidence to date.

items.title: AsyncFlow: Asynchronous Streaming RL Post-Training
items.summary: An async streaming RL framework targeting the throughput limits of synchronous/colocated RL — directly relevant to NPU constraints like the missing vLLM sleep mode.

items.title: DeepSeek-R1: Incentivizing Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Pure large-scale RL (GRPO + verifiable rewards) elicits emergent reasoning with no SFT (R1-Zero); catalyzed the 2025 RLVR wave.

items.title: GSPO: Group Sequence Policy Optimization
items.summary: Sequence-level importance ratios and clipping fix GRPO instability and notably stabilize MoE RL; contributed to Qwen3.

items.title: DAPO: An Open-Source LLM RL System at Scale
items.summary: GRPO + Clip-Higher, dynamic sampling, token-level loss, and overlong reward shaping; 50 on AIME 2024 with Qwen2.5-32B at half the steps.

items.title: ProRL: Prolonged RL Expands Reasoning Boundaries
items.summary: Stabilizes very long RL runs (KL control, reference resetting) and shows prolonged RL uncovers genuinely new reasoning strategies.

items.title: MiniMax-M1: Test-Time Compute with Lightning Attention (CISPO)
items.summary: Open hybrid-attention reasoner trained with CISPO (clips IS-weights, not tokens); full RL on 512 H800s in ~3 weeks.

items.title: AReaL: Large-Scale Asynchronous RL for Language Reasoning
items.summary: Fully decouples rollout from training with staleness-enhanced PPO for ~2.77x speedup; now ships a stable Ascend branch.

items.title: Native Sparse Attention (NSA)
items.summary: Trainable, hardware-aligned hierarchical sparse attention matching full attention with large speedups at 64K length.

items.title: The Entropy Mechanism of RL for Reasoning LLMs
items.summary: Explains policy-entropy collapse via log-prob/advantage covariance and proposes Clip-Cov / KL-Cov to sustain exploration.

items.title: Pretraining LLMs with NVFP4
items.summary: Large-scale 4-bit (NVFP4) pretraining of a 12B model on 10T tokens matching FP8 — the most ambitious FP4 training run reported.

items.title: GLM-4.5: Agentic, Reasoning, and Coding Foundation Models
items.summary: Hybrid thinking/direct MoE (355B/32B) trained with a difficulty-based RL curriculum on the open-source 'slime' framework.

items.title: Reinforcement Learning with Metacognitive Feedback Elicits Faithful Uncertainty Expression in LLMs
items.summary: Metacognition is a critical component of intelligence that describes the ability to monitor and regulate one's own cognitive processes. Yet LLMs exhibit systemic deficiencies in key metacognitive faculties: they hallucinate with high confidence, fail to recognize knowledge boundaries, and misreprese…

items.title: TRIAGE: Role-Typed Credit Assignment for Agentic Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Agentic reinforcement learning requires assigning credit to environment-facing actions such as searches, clicks, edits, navigation commands, and object interactions. Standard GRPO uses the final verifier outcome as a uniform advantage over all action tokens. This outcome signal is useful but structu…

items.title: GR2 Technical Report
items.summary: Industrial recommendation systems serve billions of users through a multi-stage funnel -- retrieval, early-stage ranking, and re-ranking -- where the final re-ranking step disproportionately shapes user engagement and downstream performance, particularly for carousel and grid display formats. Despit…

items.title: Z-1: Efficient Reinforcement Learning for Vision-Language-Action Models
items.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models offer a promising framework for robotic manipulation by connecting language instructions, visual observations, and continuous control. However, most existing policies remain limited by behavior cloning or supervised fine-tuning (SFT) from fixed demonstrations, whi…

items.title: Geometry-Preserving Orthonormal Initialization for Low-Rank Adaptation in RLVR
items.summary: Low-rank adaptation (LoRA) and its variants enable parameter-efficient fine-tuning of large language models under the supervised fine-tuning (SFT) paradigm. However, their efficacy and behavior under Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) are less well understood. In particular, two s…

items.title: UniCoder: Unified Visual-to-Code Generation via Symbolic Rewards and Reference-Guided Code Optimization
items.summary: Visual-to-Code generation, which transforms scientific plots, vector graphics, and webpages into executable scripts, demands a level of pixel-precise alignment that standard Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) fail to achieve through Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) alone. While Reinforcement Learn…

items.title: What Memory Do GUI Agents Really Need? From Passive Records to Active Task-Driving States
items.summary: Mobile GUI agents increasingly face long-horizon tasks that require reading, updating, and reusing task-relevant data across pages and applications. Existing memory methods treat memory largely as passive storage, where past observations are accumulated and retrieved when needed. Yet retrieving a va…

items.title: Which Tokens Matter? Adaptive Token Selection for RLVR with the Relative Surprisal Index
items.summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a powerful tool for propelling Large Language Models (LLMs) beyond imitation-based training towards more robust reasoning capabilities. Among existing approaches, RL with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has emerged as a pivotal paradigm for advancing LLM reasoning. D…

items.title: Learning to Select, Not Relearn: Hard-Routed Mixtures of Reasoning LoRAs
items.summary: Composing independently trained LoRA adapters into a single large language model is useful for multi-domain adaptation, especially when the original training data cannot be shared. A common approach is to use MoE-style routing over LoRA experts, but for frozen pretrained adapters, soft weighted comb…

items.title: Plan Right, Then Plan Tight: Symbolic RL for Efficient Embodied Reasoning
items.summary: Embodied task planning asks an agent to turn a natural-language instruction into an executable sequence of actions in a physical scene, and is a building block for household, assistive, and service robots. Recent prompting-based and reinforcement-learning planners generate fluent action text but lac…

items.title: Addressing Over-Refusal in LLMs with Competing Rewards
items.summary: Safety training on language models often induces over-refusal: improved safety on harmful prompts at the cost of increased refusal on harmless ones. Though this trade-off can be mitigated by training models with reinforcement learning (RL) to reason before answering, it does not remove the underlyin…

items.title: Think in English, Answer in Korean: Efficient Adaptation of Multilingual Tool-Using Agents
items.summary: We present LuckyStar 111B, a 111B-parameter hybrid reasoning model developed through a collaboration between Cohere and LG CNS for Korean-English enterprise agents under practical memory and serving constraints. The model trains from Cohere's fully post-trained Command A model rather than a new pret…

items.title: Token-Sparse Medical Multimodal Reasoning via Dual-Stream Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) combining reinforcement learning (RL) ignite remarkable progress in multimodal reasoning, yet still struggle with medical images, which typically exhibit extremely sparse visual evidence to inform clinical decision-making. We recognize that pruning visual tokens outside…

items.title: What Probing Reveals about Autonomous Driving: Linking Internal Prediction Errors to Ego Planning
items.summary: Large-scale datasets and fast simulators have enabled improvements in driving policies that appear safe and robust, yet strong performance in nominal scenarios can still mask flawed reasoning and unsafe heuristics. Summary scores from closed-loop simulators do not give significant insight into the p…

items.title: Robust Autonomous UAV Landing on Maritime Platforms via Multimodal Agentic AI and Active Wave Compensation
items.summary: Autonomous aerial inspection of marine infrastructure is frequently compromised by stochastic sea states, introducing risks of high-kinetic impacts, post-landing toppling, and sensory occlusion. This paper proposes a decoupled, multi-vehicle landing framework synchronizing an Unmanned Surface Vehicl…

items.title: Stabilization Learning: A Paradigm Transition Bridging Control Theory and Machine Learning
items.summary: Stabilization learning is an interdisciplinary paradigm that bridges control theory and machine learning. Its core idea is to enable systems to adjust their policies under perturbations or environmental changes through real-time feedback and adaptive mechanisms. It takes stability as its primary goa…

items.title: Xiaomi-GUI-0 Technical Report
items.summary: Graphical user interface (GUI) agents build on vision-language models to complete user tasks end-to-end in real applications through interface actions such as tapping, swiping, text entry, and navigation. However, existing GUI agents are trained and evaluated largely on offline trajectories, simulat…

items.title: Stage-Transition Dense Reward Modeling for Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Reinforcement learning for long-horizon robotic manipulation is often limited by sparse and delayed rewards, while manually designing dense shaping signals is costly and brittle to changes in environments and object configurations. This work proposes Stage-Transition Dense Reward (STDR), a visual re…

items.title: Failure-Based Testing for Deep Reinforcement Learning Agents
items.summary: Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) agents have been widely adopted across diverse domains to address challenging decision-making problems, such as autonomous driving and robotic control. Given that many of these applications are safety- and security-critical, rigorous testing of DRL agents is indispe…

items.title: Smart charging of large fleets of Electric Vehicles: Independent Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning approaches
items.summary: The electrification of transportation through electric vehicles introduces new challenges for power grid management, such as increased peak demand, voltage fluctuations, line overloads, and the integration of variable renewable energy sources. To enable efficient integration of EVs while minimizing …

items.title: SeKV: Resolution-Adaptive KV Cache with Hierarchical Semantic Memory for Long-Context LLM Inference
items.summary: Large language models increasingly operate over long contexts, where the KV cache becomes a dominant memory bottleneck: its size grows linearly with sequence length and must be retained throughout decoding, making full GPU caching prohibitively expensive without compression. Existing KV cache compre…

items.title: Omni-Flow: A Unified Workflow Orchestration and Distributed KV Cache Sharing Framework for Multimodal Inference
items.summary: As large language model (LLM) inference evolves from text-only to multimodal paradigms, inference systems face three challenges: (1) flexible orchestration of multimodal workflows, where heterogeneous computing units exhibit complex dependencies and concurrent control; (2) efficient transmission of …

items.title: FaceMoE: Mixture of Experts for Low-Resolution Face Recognition
items.summary: Low-resolution face recognition (LR-FR) remains a challenging task due to poor feature extraction and aggregation, as probe images often contain limited identity information resulting from extreme degradations such as blur, occlusion, and low contrast. Additionally, the domain gap between high-resol…

items.title: Mixture-of-Control: State-Aware Fine-Tuning for Transformer-based Models
items.summary: State-based fine-tuning has emerged as a compelling alternative to weight-based adaptation for transformers, updating lightweight controls into states rather than model weights, offering substantial memory savings while retaining parameter efficiency. However, most existing state-based methods typic…

items.title: ExPLoRe: Expert Patch-Level Loss Routing for Multi-Objective Masked Image Modeling
items.summary: Multi-objective masked image modeling (MIM) combines complementary learning signals (token distillation, CLS alignment, and pixel reconstruction) but existing methods weight these objectives with global scalars, ignoring spatial heterogeneity across patches. items.title: Transformers as Bayesian In-Context Experimenters: Smoothness-Adaptive Efficient ATE Estimation
items.summary: Adaptive experiments for average treatment effects (ATE) require randomized allocations balancing valid inference with statistical efficiency. The oracle design is a covariate-dependent Neyman rule governed by unknown arm-conditional outcome variances. We investigate whether this sequential variance…

items.title: ComplianceGate: Classifier-Gated Multi-Tier LLM Routing for Inference in Regulated Industries
items.summary: Large language models deployed in regulated industries operate under two constraints: compliance enforcement and cost efficiency. Personally identifiable information (PII) in user queries can reach model endpoints before the system determines whether that data should leave its jurisdictional boundar…

items.title: A Three-Phase Foundation Model for Tax-Aware Personalized Portfolio Management
items.summary: We present a three-phase deep reinforcement learning system for personalized portfolio management that addresses three limitations shared by all prior financial RL work: 1) ticker lock-in, 2) monolithic objectives , and 3) static user models. Phase 1 pretrains a ticker-identity-free cross asset enco…

items.title: Experience Augmented Policy Optimization for LLM Reasoning
items.summary: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) is a powerful paradigm for improving the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs). However, existing RLVR methods typically rely on on-policy optimization from scratch, resulting in high sampling costs and inefficient utilization of…

items.title: DRIFT: Difficulty Routing Self-DIstillation with Rhythm-Gated Exploration and Success BuFfer Training
items.summary: Enabling large language models to achieve stable self-improvement without external expert supervision remains a central challenge in complex reasoning tasks. Existing self-distillation and reinforcement learning methods lack explicit mechanisms for tracking problem-level learning progress and adapti…

items.title: Preserving Speech-to-Text LLM Capabilities in Speech-to-Speech Generation
items.summary: Strong speech-to-text (S2T) LLMs already provide robust speech perception and text reasoning, but adding speech-to-speech (S2S) output is challenging: fine-tuning the backbone can degrade the original S2T performance, while attaching a downstream talker reintroduces a serial text-to-speech bottlenec…

items.title: Morphing into Hybrid Attention Models
items.summary: Hybrid attention models improve long-context efficiency by retaining only a subset of full-attention layers and replacing the remaining layers with linear attention. However, the effectiveness of Transformer-to-hybrid conversion critically depends on which layers preserve full attention. Existing hy…

items.title: TraceLab: Characterizing Coding Agent Workloads for LLM Serving
items.summary: Coding agents are rapidly becoming a major application of agentic LLMs, but serving them efficiently remains challenging. Progress on this challenge requires understanding real workload patterns, yet the data needed for such analysis is largely absent. Existing public traces and benchmarks do not ca…

items.title: Hierarchical Global Attention (HGA)
items.summary: Hierarchical Global Attention (HGA) is a drop-in replacement for dense causal attention in pretrained long-context transformers. HGA preserves the original checkpoint parameters: the pretrained $W_Q$, $W_K$, $W_V$, and $W_O$ projections remain unchanged, no calibration parameters are introduced, and…

items.title: MuonSSM: Orthogonalizing State Space Models for Sequence Modeling
items.summary: State space models (SSMs) have emerged as efficient linear-time alternatives to attention for long-sequence modeling. However, existing SSMs often suffer from instability and memory degradation over extended horizons due to poorly conditioned first-order updates and unbalanced update geometry. We in…

items.title: Predict, Reuse, and Repair: Accelerating Dynamic Sparse Attention for Long-Context LLM Decoding
items.summary: Dynamic sparse attention (DSA) accelerates long-context LLM decoding by attending to only the top-K KV blocks relevant to each query, but it introduces a serialized selection-to-attention dependency that emerges as a new latency bottleneck. We present PRR, a speculate-reuse-repair runtime that explo…

items.title: Parametric Skills
items.summary: Since intelligence fundamentally relies on efficient skill acquisition (Chollet, 2019), the ability to leverage skills is critical. For LLMs, skills, manually authored or extracted from task trajectories, are textual recipes encoding mature problem-solving experience and are critical to agentic capa…

items.title: HBM Is Not All You Need: Efficient Disaggregated LLM Serving across Memory-heterogeneous Accelerators
items.summary: LLM inference comprises a compute-bound prefill phase and a memory-bound decode phase, and recent systems disaggregate them onto separate hardware. Yet today's datacenter GPUs rely on costly HBM whose bandwidth sits almost entirely idle during prefill. LLM serving across memory-heterogeneous acceler…

items.title: Scaling the Horizon, Not the Parameters: Reaching Trillion-Parameter Performance with a 35B Agent
items.summary: We introduce Agents-A1, a 35B Mixture-of-Experts Agentic Model that reaches trillion-parameter-level performance by scaling the agent horizon. We investigate agent-horizon scaling from two perspectives: scaling long-horizon trajectories and scaling heterogeneous agent abilities. To support this goal…

items.title: A Multi-task Mixture of Experts Framework for Malware Classification, Packing Detection, and Family Attribution
items.summary: Malware classification remains a challenging problem due to its inherent heterogeneity, the presence of packed binaries, and the diverse distribution of malware families. Traditional single-model detection mechanisms often fail to generalize across such diverse data, leading to degraded performance,…

items.title: Residual-Guided Expert Specialization for Incomplete Multimodal Learning
items.summary: As real-world prediction systems often face missing modalities at inference, incomplete multimodal learning (IML) remains a practical challenge. While prior methods aim to learn representations robust to missing inputs, representations from incomplete modalities inevitably deviate from their full-mo…

items.title: Towards Continual Motion-Language Agents: LoRA Variants for Incremental Motion Understanding and Generation
items.summary: Motion-language agents must possess the bidirectional capability to both understand human movement (motion-to-text, M2T) and generate it from natural language (text-to-motion, T2M). items.title: W4A4 Quantization for Inference on Wan2.2-I2V-A14B

items.title: MLVC: Multi-platform Learned Video Codec for Real-World Deployment
items.summary: Neural video codecs have surpassed classical codecs in coding efficiency but remain impractical for deployment due to cross-platform incompatibility and high computational cost. Existing quantization-based solutions fail to produce deterministic results across diverse hardware platforms, leading to …

items.title: Phase Matters: Characterizing Heterogeneous Vision-Language Inference on a Mobile SoC
items.summary: Recent phone-class mobile SoCs expose practical NPU execution paths for on-device vision-language model (VLM) inference, but developers still lack phase-level guidance for mapping VLM pipelines across heterogeneous backends. items.title: CANNs: A Toolkit for Research on Continuous Attractor Neural Networks
items.summary: Continuous attractor neural networks (CANNs) are the canonical computational framework for how the brain encodes continuous variables such as spatial position, head direction, and movement direction, and explain the activity of hippocampal place cells, entorhinal grid cells, and head-direction cells…

items.title: Mechanism-Driven Monitors for Preemptive Detection of LLM Training Instability

After a numerical or a hyperparameter fault has already destabilized the training dynamics, it may continue for thousands of steps while loss …

items.title: SharQ: Bridging Activation Sparsity and FP4 Quantization for LLM Inference
items.summary: Low-bit floating-point formats and semi-structured sparsity are increasingly supported by modern accelerators, yet combining them for LLM activation compression remains challenging: activations contain input-dependent outliers that dominate block scales in FP4 quantization, and directly applying N:M…

items.title: Migration of silting objects via adjoint pairs
items.summary: Consider an adjoint triple of triangle functors between two nice enough triangulated categories. In this paper, we are looking for conditions under which the silting, respectively, cosilting property ascends or descends via at most left, respectively, at most right adjoint.

items.title: FlexServe: A Fast and Secure LLM Serving System for Mobile Devices with Flexible Resource Isolation
items.summary: Device-side Large Language Models (LLMs) have grown explosively, offering stronger privacy and higher availability than their cloud-side counterparts. items.title: EnerInfer: Energy-Aware On-Device LLM Inference
items.summary: On-device LLM inference is increasingly attractive for privacy-preserving, reliable, and cost-effective deployment, yet its energy and thermal costs remain a critical bottleneck. Existing systems primarily optimize for decoding speed, implicitly assuming that faster execution is always preferable. W…

items.title: MINCE: Shrinking LLM Evaluation Datasets via Few-Model Monte Carlo Calibration
items.summary: Evaluating LLMs across many model variants -- quantized, fine-tuned, or deployment-specific -- requires running large benchmarks repeatedly, a process that can take tens of hours per model on edge hardware such as NPUs. items.summary: We present HyperQuant (Hadamard, optimallY Packing, Entropy Rice-coding), a unified post-training quantization pipeline for the weights and the KV cache of large language and diffusion transformers. Across a suite of self-contained experiments (Table 1), HyperQuant outperforms the recent HIGGS schem…

items.title: A Theory-grounded Hybrid Neural Network Integrating Complementary Estimation Mechanisms for Stable Visual Object TrackingA
items.summary: Hybrid neural networks (HNNs) that integrate artificial neural networks (ANNs) with brain-inspired neural networks have achieved broad success across perception and control tasks. However, much of the current success is confined to neuron-scale hybridization, where discrete, spike-based coding funda…

items.title: NeutronSparse: Coordinating Heterogeneous Engines for Sparse Matrix Multiplication on NPUs
items.summary: Sparse matrix-matrix multiplication (SpMM) is a fundamental data operation for large-scale sparse data processing. items.title: Clinical Term Extraction using Open-Source Small Language Models
items.summary: Clinical information for amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS) care documented in unstructured clinical notes limits downstream analysis without extraction into structured formats. Open-source small language models with few-shot prompting for detecting the presence of ALS-relevant clinical terms in pa…

items.title: An Empirical Study of OpenPangu Quantization on Ascend NPUs
items.summary: OpenPangu models are attractive targets for private and domestic large-language-model deployment, yet their robustness under aggressive post-training quantization on Ascend NPUs has not been systematically characterized. This paper conducts a controlled empirical study of OpenPangu 1B and 7B models …

items.title: Modest, artistic, and radical solutions to the environmental impact of image-generating machine learning
items.summary: Machine learning is often touted to improve the efficiency of ICT, but that small gain is overwhelmed by the enormous carbon, water, and land footprints of data centers and ML-ready devices. We survey the electricity consumption of ML applications in training and inference, focusing on electricity-i…

items.title: Towards Scalable Customization and Deployment of Multi-Agent Systems for Enterprise Applications
items.summary: Large language model (LLM)-based multi-agent systems demonstrate strong performance on complex reasoning and task execution, enabling broad enterprise applications. However, production deployment remains challenging due to domain-specific customization requirements and high latency and inference cos…

items.title: ACCORD: Action-Conditioned Contextual Grounding for Language Agents
items.summary: User instructions are often underspecified because humans rely on implicit assumptions about the surrounding environment. For large language model (LLM) agents operating in information-rich digital and physical environments, these assumptions cannot be inferred from the instruction alone; they must …
